elements.name: nve-accordion
elements.description: An accordion is a vertical stack of interactive headings used to toggle the display of further information.

properties.name: container
properties.description: flat (Borderless, container-less accordions), full (default), or inset (Rounded corner, contained accordion)

properties.name: expanded
properties.description: Determines whether the accordion is expanded, displaying its contents, or not.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Determines whether the accordion is expandable

properties.name: behaviorExpand
properties.description: Determines whether or not the accordion should opt-in to stateful expansion behavior (defaults to stateless)

slots.description: This is a default/unnamed slot for accordion content
This is a default/unnamed slot for accordion content content

properties.name: behaviorExpand
properties.description: Determines whether or not the accordion should opt-in to stateful expansion behavior (defaults to stateless)

properties.name: behaviorExpandSingle
properties.description: Determines whether or not the accordion should opt-in to stateful expansion of a single accordion at a time

properties.name: container
properties.description: flat (Borderless, container-less accordions), full (default), or inset (Rounded corner, contained accordion)

elements.name: nve-alert
elements.description: Alert is an element that displays a brief, important message in a way that attracts the user's attention without interrupting the user's task.

properties.name: status
properties.description: visual treatment to represent a ongoing task or support status

properties.name: closable
properties.description: enable closable alert when placed within a nve-alert-group

elements.name: nve-alert-banner
elements.description: An alert banner is an element that displays a brief, important message outside the context of the current page.

elements.name: nve-alert-group
elements.description: An alert group is an element that displays a group of related and important messages in a way that attracts the user's attention without interrupting the user's task.

elements.name: nve-app-header
elements.description: An element that appears across the top of all pages containing the application name and primary navigation.

slots.description: Use the default slot in `nve-logo` to create an app logo badge within the app header. Include a `<span>` element inside `nve-app-header` to change the default application title.
For `nve-button` and `nve-icon-button` elements used for navigation behavior. Use the `active` attribute to indicate the current page.
slots.name: nav-items

slots.description: For `nve-icon-button` elements. This will place them in the section of the app header where supplemental actions are located.
slots.name: nav-actions

elements.name: nve-badge
elements.description: A visual indicator that communicates a status description of an associated component. Status badges use short text, color, and icons for quick recognition and are placed near the relevant content.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Visual treatment to represent a ongoing task, support or trend status. [Figma](https://www.figma.com/file/vbcJuxNZO6t2KScQ8y5H7z/%F0%9F%93%9A-MagLev-Elements-Design-Catalog---WIP?type=design&node-id=48-710&mode=design)

properties.name: color
properties.description: Highlights content to draw attention and convey simple messages. [Figma](https://www.figma.com/file/vbcJuxNZO6t2KScQ8y5H7z/%F0%9F%93%9A-MagLev-Elements-Design-Catalog---WIP?type=design&node-id=56-308&mode=design&t=eeBXUleEIUIAeQQh-0)

elements.name: nve-breadcrumb
elements.description: Breadcrumb is a component that can help users establish their location while navigating a website with complex URLs and navigation paths.

elements.name: nve-button
elements.description: A button is a widget that enables users to trigger an action or event, such as submitting a form, opening a dialog, canceling an action, or performing a delete operation.

properties.name: interaction
properties.description: Visual treatment to represent user interaction appearance

properties.name: size
properties.description: Determines size of button

properties.name: pressed
properties.description:
Use for buttons that are toggle button types.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-pressed

properties.name: expanded
properties.description:
Use for button that are used for expanding/collapsing content.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-expanded

properties.name: readonly
properties.description:
Similar to input readonly, sets a button sematically as visual treatment only
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/readonly

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name of the button, submitted as a pair with the button's value as part of the form data, when that button is used to submit the form.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button#attr-name

properties.name: value
properties.description:
Defines the value associated with the button's name when it's submitted with the form data. This value is passed to the server in params when the form is submitted using this button.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button#attr-value

properties.name: type
properties.description:
Defines the button behavior when associated within an <form> element.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button#attr-type

properties.name: disabled
properties.description:
This Boolean attribute prevents the user from interacting with the button: it cannot be pressed or focused.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button#attr-disabled

properties.name: selected
properties.description:
This Boolean attribute prevents the selected state if button is part of a multi choice selection group
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-selected

slots.description: slot for button text content or icon, icon placement determined by whether <nve-icon> is inserted before or after text content.

elements.name: nve-button-group
elements.description: A button group is a control that enables users to choose between two or more distinct mutually exclusive options.

properties.name: behaviorSelect
properties.description: By default the button group is stateless. Add the `behavior-select` attribute and set to `single` or `multi` to enable stateful selction handling.

properties.name: container
properties.description: Set the style of the button group using the `container` property. Options are the default display when the attribute is left off, `flat` or `rounded`.

properties.name: orientation
properties.description: Determines the orientation direction of the group. Vertical groups are limited to icon buttons only.

properties.name: interaction
properties.description: The `interaction` property is intended to be used on `nve-button-group` in combination with `nve-divider` for color-coded split buttons

properties.name: size
properties.description: Determines size of button

elements.name: nve-card
elements.description: A container for content representing a single entity.

properties.name: container
properties.description: flat (embed into parent container) or full (full parent container width)

slots.description: This is a default/unnamed slot for card content
This is a default/unnamed slot for card content content
This is a default/unnamed slot for card footer content

elements.name: nve-checkbox
elements.description: A checkbox is a control that enables users to choose between two distinct mutually exclusive options (checked or unchecked, on or off) through a single click or tap.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Set current visaul state for control. This overrides any validation states active.

properties.name: layout
properties.description: Set current control + label + control message layout

properties.name: fitText
properties.description: Sets the input to match the width of the active text content of the control value. Only applicable to vertical input box type controls (input, select)

properties.name: fitContent
properties.description: Sets the input to match native default content block

properties.name: reset
properties.description: resets control value to initial attribute value and clears any active validation rules

properties.name: layout
properties.description: Set each slotted control + label + control message layout

elements.description: Defining a Validity State on a `nve-control-message` will allow messages to be shown conditionally based on the current HTML5 validity state.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Visual treatment for current form control validation status

properties.name: error
properties.description:
Validation error code for current form control
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ValidityState

elements.name: nve-date
elements.description: A date picker is a control that enables users to choose a date value.

elements.name: nve-dialog
elements.description: Dialog is a component that appears above main content. A modal dialog is used to display critical information that requires users attention. Use `hidden` to show or hide the dialog.

properties.name: position
properties.description:
Sets the side position of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.
For dialog the anchor defaults to the document body.

properties.name: alignment
properties.description:
Sets the alignment of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.
If an arrow exists the alginment will be relative to the arrow against the anchor.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Sets the maximum size of the dialog.

properties.name: closable
properties.description:
Determines if a close button should render within dialog. Non-closable
dialogs can be used for dialogs that require user confirmation steps.

properties.name: modal
properties.description: Determines if a dialog should have a modal backdrop that visually overlays the UI.

properties.name: anchor
properties.description:
The anchor provides the element that the popover should position relative to.
Anchor can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: trigger
properties.description:
The trigger defines what element triggers an `open` interaction event.
A trigger can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: hidden
properties.description: Determines if popover should be rendered and positioned.

slots.description: default slot for the dialog footer
default slot for the dialog header

elements.name: nve-divider
elements.description: Divider is a component that separates and distinguishes sections of content or groups of menuitems.

elements.name: nve-dot
elements.description: A visual indicator that communicates a status or notification of an associated component.

properties.name: status
properties.description: visual treatment to represent a ongoing task status

properties.name: size
properties.description: determines size of dot relative to provided text

elements.name: nve-drawer
elements.description: Drawer are to display content that is out of context of the rest of the page (notifications, navigations, settings). Alternatively [Panel](./?path=/docs/elements-panel-documentation--docs) is inline as its content is coupled or closely related to the content on the page (details, additional actions/options).

properties.name: position
properties.description:
Sets the side position of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.
For drawer the anchor defaults to the document body.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Sets the maximum size of the drawer.

properties.name: closable
properties.description:
Determines if a close button should render within drawer. Non-closable
drawers can be used for drawers that require user confirmation steps.

properties.name: modal
properties.description: Determines if a drawer should have a modal backdrop that visually overlays the UI.

properties.name: anchor
properties.description:
The anchor provides the element that the popover should position relative to.
Anchor can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.
Drawers should default to the document body.

properties.name: trigger
properties.description:
The trigger defines what element triggers an `open` interaction event.
A trigger can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: inline
properties.description:
Create a inline layout effect while retaining the a11y characteristics and top layer behavior (light dismiss, focus trap, non interactive background)
https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/

properties.name: hidden
properties.description: Determines if popover should be rendered and positioned.

slots.description: default slot for the drawer footer
default slot for the drawer header

elements.name: nve-dropdown
elements.description: Generic dropdown element for rendering a variety of different content within a dropdown container.

properties.name: anchor
properties.description:
The anchor provides the element that the popover should position relative to.
Anchor can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: trigger
properties.description:
The trigger defines what element triggers an `open` interaction event.
A trigger can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: position
properties.description: Sets the side position of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.

properties.name: alignment
properties.description:
Sets the alignment of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.
If an arrow exists the alginment will be relative to the arrow against the anchor.

properties.name: closable
properties.description:
Determines if a close button should render within dropdown. Non-closable
dropdowns can be used for menu or selection patterns.

properties.name: arrow
properties.description: Determines if an arrow should be rendered.

properties.name: hidden
properties.description: Determines if popover should be rendered and positioned.

elements.name: nve-file
elements.description: A file picker is a control that enables users to choose a file value.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Set current visaul state for control. This overrides any validation states active.

properties.name: layout
properties.description: Set current control + label + control message layout

properties.name: fitText
properties.description: Sets the input to match the width of the active text content of the control value. Only applicable to vertical input box type controls (input, select)

properties.name: fitContent
properties.description: Sets the input to match native default content block

properties.name: reset
properties.description: resets control value to initial attribute value and clears any active validation rules

elements.name: nve-grid
elements.description: A grid widget is a container that enables users to navigate the information or interactive elements it contains using directional navigation keys, such as arrow keys, Home, and End. As a generic container widget that offers flexible keyboard navigation, it can serve a wide variety of needs. It can be used for purposes as simple as grouping a collection of checkboxes or navigation links or as complex as creating a full-featured spreadsheet application. - ARIA Authoring Practices Guide

properties.name: container
properties.description: flat (embed into parent container) or full (full parent container width)

properties.name: stripe
properties.description: determine style variant stripe rows

properties.name: width
properties.description: specify exact width using css value types

elements.name: nve-icon
elements.description: An icon is a graphic symbol designed to visually indicate the purpose of an interface element.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Visual treatment to represent a ongoing task, support or trend status.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Controls the bounding box size of the icon given a t-shirt style value.

properties.name: direction
properties.description: Sets the direction of the icon. Only supported by expand-panel/collapse-panel (horizontal axis) and arrow/caret/chevron icons (4-directions)

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the icon SVG sprite

elements.name: nve-icon-button
elements.description: An icon button is a button that displays only an icon without a visual label.

Sets the direction of the icon.

properties.name: expanded
properties.description: expand all nodes

elements.name: nve-logo

properties.name: color

properties.name: size

elements.name: nve-menu
elements.description: A menu is a widget that offers a list of choices to the user, such as a set of actions or functions. Menu widgets behave like native operating system menus, such as the menus that pull down from the menubars commonly found at the top of many desktop application windows. A menu is usually opened, or made visible, by activating a menu button, choosing an item in a menu that opens a sub menu, or by invoking a command, such as Shift + F10 in Windows, that opens a context specific menu. - ARIA Authoring Practices Guide

properties.name: selected

elements.name: nve-notification
elements.description: Displays real time updates without interrupting the user's workflow to communicate an important message or status.

properties.name: anchor
properties.description:
The anchor provides the element that the popover should position relative to.
Anchor can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: trigger
properties.description:
The trigger defines what element triggers an `open` interaction event.
A trigger can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: position
properties.description: Sets the side position of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.

properties.name: alignment
properties.description: Sets the alignment of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.

properties.name: closable
properties.description: Determines if a close button should render within notification.

properties.name: closeTimeout
properties.description: A delayed `close` event will occur determined from the provided millisecond value.

properties.name: hidden
properties.description: Determines if popover should be rendered and positioned.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Determines the visual status of the notification.

elements.name: nve-notification-group
elements.description: Displays real time updates without interrupting the user's workflow to communicate an important message or status.

elements.name: nve-page-loader
elements.description: Page Loader is a full-screen version of the nve-progress-ring component, for use when the page should remain unusable as the loader is displayed.

elements.name: nve-pagination
elements.description: Pagination is a control that enables users to navigate through pages of content.

elements.name: nve-panel
elements.description: Panel is inline container for content that is coupled to the content on the page (details, additional actions/options). Alternatively [Drawer](./?path=/docs/elements-drawer-documentation--docs) is out of context of the rest of the page (notifications, navigations, settings).

properties.name: expanded
properties.description: Determines whether or not the panel is fully expanded, displaying its contents, or not.

properties.name: closable
properties.description: Determines whether or not the panel will collapse down to an expand icon, or fully hide.

properties.name: behaviorExpand
properties.description: Determines whether or not the panel should handle auto-closing behavior vs. defaults to off.

properties.name: side
properties.description: Sets the proper collapse icon and collapse animation, based on what side of the page the panel will be used.

slots.description: This is a default/unnamed slot for panel content
This is a default/unnamed slot for panel content content
This is a default/unnamed slot for panel footer content

elements.name: nve-password
elements.description: A password is a control that enables users to enter password text.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Set current visaul state for control. This overrides any validation states active.

properties.name: layout
properties.description: Set current control + label + control message layout

properties.name: fitText
properties.description: Sets the input to match the width of the active text content of the control value. Only applicable to vertical input box type controls (input, select)

properties.name: fitContent
properties.description: Sets the input to match native default content block

properties.name: reset
properties.description: resets control value to initial attribute value and clears any active validation rules

elements.name: nve-progress-bar
elements.description: A progress bar is a visual indicator of the status of a running task. Under the hood the native HTML `progress` element is used to achieve proper a11y concerns.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current `value` of the progress indicator, set to the native HTML progress element.

properties.name: max
properties.description: The `max` property sets the highest value that `value` will be scaled to.

elements.name: nve-progress-ring
elements.description: The `nve-progress-ring` component is used to indicate the status of a pending task. It also serves the basis of the page loading element.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current `value` of the progress ring. When not set, an indeterminate animation will show.

properties.name: max
properties.description: The `max` value of the progress ring that the `value` is proportionaly scaled to.

properties.name: status
properties.description: There are four visual treatments that represent the `status` of varius tasks. When `status` is set to `warning`, `success` or `danger`, appropriate icons are embedded.

properties.name: size
properties.description: T-shirt `size` of the progress indicator, used to scale the ring.

elements.name: nve-progressive-filter-chip
elements.description: A filter chip is a control that enables users to select multiple options from a set of choices.

properties.name: closable
properties.description: Determines if a close button should render within filter chip

elements.name: nve-radio
elements.description: A radio button is a control that enables users to choose one option from a list of mutually exclusive options.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Set current visaul state for control. This overrides any validation states active.

properties.name: layout
properties.description: Set current control + label + control message layout

properties.name: fitText
properties.description: Sets the input to match the width of the active text content of the control value. Only applicable to vertical input box type controls (input, select)

properties.name: fitContent
properties.description: Sets the input to match native default content block

properties.name: reset
properties.description: resets control value to initial attribute value and clears any active validation rules

properties.name: layout
properties.description: Set each slotted control + label + control message layout

elements.name: nve-range
elements.description: A range slider is a control that enables users to choose a value from a continuous range of values.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Set current visaul state for control. This overrides any validation states active.

properties.name: layout

properties.name: fitText

elements.name: nve-search

properties.name: status

properties.name: layout

elements.name: nve-sort-button
elements.description: A sort button is a control that enables users to sort a list of items in ascending or descending order.

properties.name: pressed
properties.description:
Use for buttons that are toggle button types.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-pressed

properties.name: expanded
properties.description:
Use for button that are used for expanding/collapsing content.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-expanded

properties.name: readonly
properties.description:
Similar to input readonly, sets a button sematically as visual treatment only
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/readonly

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name of the button, submitted as a pair with the button's value as part of the form data, when that button is used to submit the form.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button#attr-name

properties.name: value
properties.description:
Defines the value associated with the button's name when it's submitted with the form data. This value is passed to the server in params when the form is submitted using this button.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button#attr-value

properties.name: type
properties.description:
Defines the button behavior when associated within an <form> element.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button#attr-type

properties.name: disabled
properties.description:
This Boolean attribute prevents the user from interacting with the button: it cannot be pressed or focused.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button#attr-disabled

properties.name: selected
properties.description:
This Boolean attribute prevents the selected state if button is part of a multi choice selection group
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-selected

elements.name: nve-switch
elements.description: A switch is a control that enables users to switch between two mutually exclusive options (on or off, checked or unchecked) through a single click or tap.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Set current visaul state for control. This overrides any validation states active.

properties.name: layout
properties.description: Set current control + label + control message layout

properties.name: fitText
properties.description: Sets the input to match the width of the active text content of the control value. Only applicable to vertical input box type controls (input, select)

properties.name: fitContent
properties.description: Sets the input to match native default content block

properties.name: reset
properties.description: resets control value to initial attribute value and clears any active validation rules

elements.name: nve-tabs
elements.description: Tabs provide a selection UX, typically used for swapping content shown on a page, or within a navigation context.

properties.name: vertical
properties.description: Determines whether or not the tabs should display in a vertical layout vs. defaulting to horizontal.

properties.name: borderless
properties.description: Determines whether or not the tabs should display a border on selected items vs. defaults to show border.

properties.name: behaviorSelect
properties.description: Determines whether or not the tabs should handle selection behavior vs. defaults to off.

properties.name: selected
properties.description: Determines which tab item is selected, defaults to false.

properties.description:
Use for buttons that are toggle button types.

elements.name: nve-toast
elements.description: A contextual popup that displays a status. Toasts are [triggered](https://w3c.github.io/aria/#tooltip) by clicking, focusing, or tapping an element and cannot have interactive elements within them.

properties.name: anchor
properties.description:
The anchor provides the element that the popover should position relative to.
Anchor can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: trigger
properties.description:
The trigger defines what element triggers an `open` interaction event.
A trigger can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: position
properties.description: Sets the side position of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.

properties.name: alignment
properties.description: Sets the alignment of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.

properties.name: closable
properties.description: Determines if a close button should render within toast.

properties.name: closeTimeout
properties.description: A delayed `close` event will occur determined from the provided millisecond value.

properties.name: hidden
properties.description: Determines if popover should be rendered and positioned.

properties.name: status
properties.description: visual treatment to represent a ongoing task or support status

elements.name: nve-toolbar
elements.description: A toolbar is a container for grouping a set of controls, such as buttons, icon buttons and combobox search.

properties.name: container
properties.description: Determines the container visual style.

properties.name: content
properties.description: Determines the primary content overflow behavior.

properties.name: orientation
properties.description: Determines the orientation direction of the toolbar. Vertical toolbars are limited to icon buttons only.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Visual treatment to represent accent color for interactive selections

elements.name: nve-tooltip
elements.description: A contextual popup that displays a description for an element. Tooltips are [triggered](https://w3c.github.io/aria/#tooltip) by hovering, focusing, or tapping an element and cannot have interactive elements within them.

properties.name: anchor
properties.description:
The anchor provides the element that the popover should position relative to.
Anchor can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: trigger
properties.description:
The trigger defines what element triggers an `open` interaction event.
A trigger can accept a idref string within the same render root or a HTMLElement DOM reference.

properties.name: position
properties.description: Sets the side position of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.

properties.name: alignment
properties.description: Sets the alignment of the popover relative to the provided anchor element.

properties.name: status
properties.description: visual treatment to represent a ongoing task or support status

properties.name: hidden
properties.description: Determines if popover should be rendered and positioned.
